skill.name: open-access-scout
skill.description: Use when finding open access journals, checking journal policies, or identifying predatory publishers. Helps researchers locate legitimate open access venues and avoid publication scams.
skill.summary: Helps researchers find legitimate open access journals and avoid predatory publishers.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 65 static analysis findings are false positives after manual evaluation. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' (3 instances) and 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' (33 instances) findings come from markdown documentation and example code blocks in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md, not from executable code. The 'system reconnaissance' (13 instances) and 'hardcoded URL' findings (10 instances) reflect the legitimate research function of searching open access journals, using well-known academic database URLs (Unpaywall, PubMed Central, arXiv). The Python script (scripts/main.py) uses only mock data, makes no real network requests, and executes no external commands. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or business logic threats were identified.

content.user_title: Find Legitimate Open Access Journals for Your Research
content.value_statement: Researchers often struggle to identify legitimate open access journals and avoid predatory publishers. This skill helps locate verified open access venues, check publisher credibility, and compare article processing charges.
actual_capabilities: Search for open access versions of paywalled papers using DOI or title
Identify predatory publisher warning signs and red flags
Compare article processing charges across different journals
Verify journal indexing status in major academic databases
Check publisher credibility and editorial board transparency
Find preprint versions of papers on repositories like arXiv and PubMed Central
limitations: Does not perform real-time API lookups without configured script execution
Cannot guarantee complete coverage of all open access repositories
Journal policies and APC data may change after the knowledge cutoff date
Predatory check results are informational, not a definitive certification

use_cases.title: Find a Legitimate Open Access Journal for Your Manuscript
use_cases.description: A researcher needs to find a reputable open access journal in their field that fits within their publication budget and timeline.
use_cases.target_user: Early-career researcher preparing their first manuscript submission

use_cases.title: Verify a Publisher Credibility Before Submitting
use_cases.description: A scientist receives an unsolicited email invitation to publish and wants to check if the journal and publisher are legitimate.
use_cases.target_user: Academic researcher evaluating a publication invitation

use_cases.title: Compare Open Access Publication Costs
use_cases.description: A research group wants to compare article processing charges across multiple journals in their specialty to plan their publication budget.
use_cases.target_user: Research group leader planning publication budgets

prompt_templates.title: Find Open Access Journals for Your Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for open access journals in [field of study]. List journals that accept submissions on [research topic] and describe their basic credibility markers.
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner researcher looking for suitable publication venues in their field.

prompt_templates.title: Check if a Journal Is Legitimate
prompt_templates.prompt: I received an invitation from [journal name]. Check if this journal is legitimate by reviewing its editorial board, indexing status, peer review practices, and any predatory warning signs.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher evaluating a suspicious journal invitation.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Publication Costs Across Journals
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare article processing charges for [topic area] journals. List journals below $[amount] that are PubMed indexed and use double-blind peer review.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher on a limited publication budget comparing journal options.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Your Open Access Publication Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to publish my paper on [topic] with these constraints: maximum APC of $[amount], must be indexed in [database], and needs fast peer review. Identify the best matching journals and rank them by credibility score.
prompt_templates.scenario: An experienced researcher with specific publication requirements.

output_examples.input: Find open access journals for cancer immunotherapy research with APC under $3000
output: Journal of Immunotherapy of Cancer - APC: $2,500. Indexed in PubMed, Scopus. Peer review: double blind. Impact factor: 10.9.
Cancer Immunology Research - APC: $2,800. Indexed in PubMed, Web of Science. Peer review: single blind. Impact factor: 8.7.
Frontiers in Immunology - APC: $2,900. Indexed in PubMed, Scopus. Peer review: review type. Impact factor: 7.3.
Note: Always verify current APC and indexing status on the publisher website before submitting.

output_examples.input: Check if the Journal of Clinical and Medical Research is a predatory journal
output: Red flags detected: Promises 3-day review time, no clear editorial board, APC of $3,500 is above average for this field, not indexed in PubMed or Scopus, editorial board members appear unaffiliated with named institutions.
Recommendation: Avoid submitting to this journal. Consider verified alternatives in the same field.

best_practices: Always verify journal information on the publisher official website before making submission decisions.
Cross-check indexing status using major databases like PubMed, Scopus, and Web of Science directly.
Review at least three different journals to compare policies, APCs, and credibility before choosing.
anti_patterns: Relying on a single source to determine if a journal is legitimate or predatory.
Submitting to a journal based only on low APC without verifying peer review quality and indexing.
Using outdated journal information without confirming current policies, APCs, or indexing status.

faq.question: Can this skill tell me definitively if a journal is predatory?
faq.answer: No. The skill identifies common warning signs but cannot provide a definitive predatory classification. Always use multiple verification sources.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to live journal databases?
faq.answer: The script uses mock data and does not make live API calls to journal databases. Information is based on available knowledge at the time of use.

faq.question: What is an article processing charge or APC?
faq.answer: APC is the fee charged by open access journals to make an article freely available online. Fees vary widely by journal, field, and publisher.

faq.question: How can I verify if a journal is indexed in PubMed?
faq.answer: Check the NLM Catalog directly at the National Library of Medicine website for the most current indexing information.

faq.question: What are common warning signs of a predatory publisher?
faq.answer: Common signs include guaranteed rapid publication, no clear editorial board, excessive APCs, spam email invitations, and fake impact factors.

faq.question: Can this skill find open access versions of papers I need to read?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can search for free open access versions of paywalled papers using the paper title or DOI.
